Reverted 3 edits by [[Special:Contributions/Gitz6666|Gitz6666]] ([[User talk:Gitz6666|talk]]): Not improvements. Removal of sources content without valid reason.
c/e - this now logically belongs to this paragraph on governments, etc.

Cited sources don't mention "Government bodies and other institutions"; they speak about popular perception, anti-cult movements, and scholars Kent and Beit-Hallahmi (which are already covered in the section)

The cited source (Urban 2021, p. 166) says "A great many aspects of Scientology are shrouded in layers of secrecy, concealment, obfuscation, and/ or dissimulation". Exactly the same point conveyed by the preceding sentence "Much basic information about the Scientology belief system is kept secret from most practitioners", which in fact also cites Urban 2021, p. 166.
trim "law lords" as term will be understood by few international readers. ref to debate.
so we return to the status quo, not your preferred version.
[[WP:VNOT|Verifiability does not guarantee inclusion]]. Your 23/11/2025 edit doesn't have consensus on t/p, so please follow [[WP:ONUS]] and [[WP:BRD]] and seek consensus for your terminology
Restored revision 1325323057 by [[Special:Contributions/Gitz6666|Gitz6666]] ([[User talk:Gitz6666|talk]]): Reliable sources state as much. We do not censor historical details in reliable sources.

Two uses of "ridicule(d)" in the lead is too much. Removed per WP:IMPARTIAL, WP:VOICE, WP:COLLOQUIAL. This is not how encyclopedias are written. Also, I doubt the ''whole'' scientific community ridiculed Hubbard - most probably didn’t even know who he was
(rv) Definitely not my preferred wording, but there seems to be a clear consensus on the talk page for this version
Thanks for clarifying exact nature of source

WP:UNDUE - weak and old sources. Please restore with better sources
(rv) Lord Denning calling Scientology a cult doesn't support "dangerous cult" and "manipulative profit-making". Removed per WP:SYNTH

Source removed on fabricated grounds. High court judgment repeatedly characterises subject as a cult. Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1325073909|1325073909]] by [[Special:Contributions/Gitz6666|Gitz6666]] ([[User talk:Gitz6666|talk]])
Removed "Hubbard v Vosper" (1971): a copyright case that doesn't say anything about Scientology's religious status.

Removing Hansard 1996 - another misrepresented source. This is a minister's statement in a debate at the House of Lords, in which [[Emily Blatch, Baroness Blatch|Baroness Blatch]] refers to Scientology as a "cult" and warns against the dangers; this is not a government inquiry or an international parliamentary body describing Scientology as a "dangerous cult".
Recommandation 1412 (1999) "Activités illégales des sectes" does not mention Scientology
Removed Cottrell (1984): a working paper by one MEP, not a government inquiry or an international parliamentary body.
Ditto (rermoved more references to 1992 speech by Mr. Espersen)

Removed 1968 Written Answer by [[Kenneth Robinson (British politician)|Kenneth Robinson]], Minister of Health. This was a ministerial reply, not a finding of a government inquiry or parliamentary body. Removed as misattributed and UNDUE

Corrected mistake. Rec. 1178 (1992) does not mention Scientology; the quote “a cool, cynical, manipulating business…” comes from a 1992 CoE Parliamentary Assembly debate (speech by Mr. Espersen, Denmark), not from the Recommendation itself. Mr. Espersen's views are WP:UNDUE - removed source and text

Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1324796662|1324796662]] by [[Special:Contributions/Gitz6666|Gitz6666]] ([[User talk:Gitz6666|talk]])  I argue strongly on talk that IS a religion, but the quote is another mainstream point of view that's well-sourced

Long a point of contention, with editors moving “scam” to be first etc. no logic to this order. Alphabetical order simple and neutral.
c/e - "scholarship in skepticism" is awkward, plus avoiding repetitions of scholar/scholarship
Reordered content to improve flow and avoid jumping back and forth. No text or sources were added or removed

Replaced "invented" with the more neutral "created", reorded the list of definitions to follow logical progression (religion - cult - business - scam), and removed citations bundle, which does not support "religion" and is already included in the lead

Removed reference: [[J. Gordon Melton]] doesn't say that Scientology is not a religion. His 2009 article is literally titled "Birth of a Religion"; his 2000 book "The Church of Scientology" discusses the debate over Scientology's religious status without taking a position.

Willms actually argues that Scientology is a religion ("Scientology's claim of being a religion can hardly be denied from a scientific point of view", p. 245; "This does not mean that Scientology cannot be taken seriously as a religion at all. Quite the opposite", p. 257). Moved the citation to support the appropriate text in article
"75 million", as elsewhere reported in the article + source (Bromley)

ostensible reasoning for template is yet again a complete fabrication: "perceived as core" is 5 pages and 2 subchapters later than page cited, on different topic. don't misrepresent sources in maintenance templates. nevertheless, + further refs from existing source list
added {{failed verification span}} – source misrepresented; Rothstein discusses perceptions, not doctrine. Other scholars do not frame OT III as core ([[WP:V]], [[WP:RS]])
